title: Why Zillow is Ready to 'Press Down on the Accelerator' in 2024
text: Zillow's 2024 Strategy and Outlook. Core Strategy: The "Housing Super App": Zillow is positioning itself as an integrated housing platform combining multiple services. CEO Rich Barton stated the company is "pressing down on the accelerator" to expand the breadth and depth of products across more markets. The company views 2024 as a year of expansion following 2023's product rollouts. Key Growth Plans: Enhanced Markets Expansion: Zillow plans to grow its integrated homebuying experience from 9 markets to 40 by year-end. These markets combine Premier Agents and Zillow Home Loans into a unified experience. Mortgage Business Growth: Despite industry headwinds, Zillow Home Loans saw origination volume double year-over-year. In pilot markets, customer adoption rates jumped from 6% to 15% throughout 2023. Rental Market Focus: Rentals emerged as a designated growth priority, with fourth-quarter revenues up 37% annually to $93 million. Rentals now represent 20% of total company revenue, reflecting Zillow's shift away from buy-side-dominated revenue streams. Market Outlook: Barton predicted double-digit revenue growth for 2024, expressing confidence despite a tepid housing market. He dismissed competitive concerns from rivals' marketing spending, emphasizing that product quality remains the decisive factor in market leadership.
